what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment tied to a channel memory entry, linking to an external image asset via a URI-style target.
observations: YAML frontmatter only with no body content
standouts: Target uses an image:// URI scheme pointing to design-source/recent-pitches/p-22
File is purely metadata with no descriptive caption or alt text
conventions: Follows a YAML frontmatter schema with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
concerns: No alt text, caption, or content description for the image
URI target cannot be verified from this file alone
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that links an image asset to the design-source channel without describing its contents.
questions: What does the image at image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-22 actually depict?
Who added this attachment and for what purpose?
Is the image:// URI resolvable by the consuming system?
purpose_vs_actual: The implicit purpose is to catalog an image attachment; the file does exactly that, though it provides minimal metadata.
intention_signals: It appears designed to attach a pitch-related image to the design-source channel so it can be retrieved alongside related memory/context.
novelty: The use of an image:// URI scheme for the target is the most distinctive feature; otherwise the structure is a conventional metadata record.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed link between a channel memory entry and an external image asset, supporting image retrieval and provenance tracking.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment record named att-b06af44b.md in the design-source channel that points to image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-22 with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to register an image attachment for a channel memory entry.
